---
title: ""
pagetitle: "drafts"
title-block-style: none
---

[Temporal Symmetries of Inquiry](https://philpapers.org/rec/CARTSO-92). (with Nate Lauffer)
]

*Holding evidence fixed, whether a proposition is about the past or about the future does not matter to whether one is rationally permitted to inquire about it.* 

[Modeling Future Indeterminacy in Possibility Semantics](https://philpapers.org/archive/CARMFI.pdf)

*What would happen if instead of building semantics for the open future in terms of overlapping complete possible worlds, we did so on the basis of evolving incomplete possibilities?*



### Coming soon


From Modal Logic to Modal Semantics. (commissioned for the Cambridge Handbook of Modality, Arregui, Hacquard and Ippolito eds.)
